title: Early Experimentation
alt: Prototype disc fitted with vertical wooden dowels during an early studio sound and form experiment
caption: In an early studio session, Tim tests the relationship between sound and spatial form using a prototype disc fitted with vertical wooden dowels. This first iteration explored the placement of sound-emitting elements and laid the conceptual groundwork for Drift's kinetic structure.

title: Cutting the Rings
alt: Large plywood rings being routed in the workshop
caption: Tim shapes large circular rings from plywood in his workshop, using a router to cut the concentric forms that would become Drift's signature visual element. The rings — some nearly four feet across — required precision cutting across multiple sessions.

title: The Rings
alt: Freshly cut concentric plywood rings nested within the original sheet
caption: The freshly cut rings, still nested within the plywood sheet from which they were routed, photographed outside the workshop. This image captures both the material process and the emerging visual language of the work: concentric circles that cast dramatic shadows in gallery space.

title: Rings in Space
alt: Suspended wooden rings casting concentric shadows during an early lighting test
caption: An early suspension test: wooden rings hung from wires in a darkened space, casting concentric shadow circles on the wall behind them. Testing how the rings interact with light became central to developing Drift's visual experience.

title: Cutting the Legs
alt: Wooden dowels being cut to size for the hanging interior structure
caption: Tim cuts the wooden dowels that will hang vertically within Drift's structure. Each rod is sized to precise dimensions to create the layered visual rhythm of the final installation.

title: Diagramming the Rotation
alt: Digital overhead schematic of the base motor and belt-drive system
caption: A digital schematic of the base's motor and belt-drive system, viewed from above. Tim developed this diagram to work out servo motor placement, pulley routing, and mounting points — the engineering logic that will make the sculpture move.

title: Building the Base
alt: Large circular base framework being glued and clamped together
caption: Tim glues and clamps the radial rib framework of the large circular base — a wagon-wheel structure that will house the drive mechanism and anchor the hanging vertical elements above.

title: The Bent Skirt
alt: Curved plywood exterior band beside the assembled base framework
caption: The curved exterior band — a strip of plywood bent into a full circle — sits beside the assembled rib framework. Getting the curve to hold cleanly required careful wood preparation and extended clamping time.

title: Securing the Base
alt: Fasteners being drilled into the assembled circular base while clamps remain in place
caption: Tim drills fasteners into the assembled base, with glue and bar clamps still in place. The structure is nearly five feet in diameter, built to support the full weight of the kinetic installation.

title: The Base Complete
alt: Finished circular plywood base with white-painted edge ready for motor installation
caption: The finished base: a smooth plywood disc with a clean white-painted edge, standing nearly five feet across. Structurally complete, it is now ready to receive the motor and electronics.

title: Electronics and Motion
alt: Servo motors, brackets, wiring, and a wooden bearing housing laid out for assembly
caption: Tate lays out the servo motors, brackets, and control wiring that will drive Drift's rotation. The stacked wooden bearing housing at center, surrounded by motor components, represents the integration of Carson's electronics and software work with Murray's woodcraft.

title: Full Installation Design
alt: Scale study rendering showing the installation footprint relative to a standing viewer
caption: Tim's scale study for the Drift installation, created to explore the work's footprint and proportions in relation to a viewer's body. The fabrication is ongoing — this rendering helps calibrate the physical form as the components come together.
